context:
Section 5 (Concluding Remarks and Future Directions), page 30 (PDF page 29 of 32 in the provided parsed text pagination)

Another important direction is to extend our smoothing frameworks to settings where the lower-level constraint set is not fixed but instead depends on the upper-level variable x. A preliminary approach based on quadratic regularization combined with penalization has been proposed in [Liu et al., 2023]; however, it remains an open question whether the resulting approximation qualifies as a smoothing family in the sense of Definition 2.5 and whether it satisfies gradient consistency.
title: Verify smoothing-family and gradient-consistency of quadratic-regularization-plus-penalization for value functions with \,\(x\)-dependent lower-level feasible sets
background_latex:
Let \(X\subseteq\mathbb{R}^n\) be a closed convex set. For each \(x\in X\), let \(Y(x)\subseteq\mathbb{R}^m\) be a nonempty closed set (a lower-level feasible set that depends on \(x\)). Let \(g: \mathbb{R}^n\times\mathbb{R}^m\to\mathbb{R}\) be a (possibly nonsmooth) function that is finite on the relevant domain, and define the associated value function
\[
 v(x):=\min_{y\in Y(x)} g(x,y),\qquad x\in X.
\]
Fix an open set \(O_X\subseteq\mathbb{R}^n\) with \(X\subseteq O_X\). Let \(\partial v(x)\) denote the Clarke subdifferential of \(v\) at \(x\) (when \(v\) is locally Lipschitz).

For \(\mu>0\), let \(v_\mu:O_X\to\mathbb{R}\) be a continuously differentiable approximation of \(v\) constructed by a scheme that combines quadratic regularization in \(y\) with a penalty term that enforces \(y\in Y(x)\) (as proposed in Liu et al. (2023) for \(x\)-dependent lower-level constraints). Denote its gradient by \(\nabla v_\mu(x)\).
problem_definition_latex:
Prove (or disprove) that the quadratic-regularization-plus-penalization approximation \(\{v_\mu\}_{\mu>0}\) for the \(x\)-dependent-set value function \(v(x)=\min_{y\in Y(x)} g(x,y)\) is
- a smoothing family of \(v\) in the sense that for every \(\bar x\in O_X\), \(\lim_{x\to \bar x,\,\mu\downarrow 0} v_\mu(x)=v(\bar x)\), and
- gradient consistent in the sense that for every \(\bar x\in O_X\),
\[
\emptyset\neq \limsup_{x\to\bar x,\,\mu\downarrow 0} \nabla v_\mu(x)\subseteq \partial v(\bar x).
\]
(Here \(\limsup_{x\to\bar x,\,\mu\downarrow 0} \nabla v_\mu(x)\) is the set of all accumulation points of gradients \(\nabla v_{\mu_k}(x_k)\) along sequences \(x_k\to\bar x\), \(\mu_k\downarrow 0\).)
